name: Gemini 3.5 Flash

price.provider: Google AI Studio / Vertex AI (Gemini API)

note: First Flash to beat the prior-gen Pro flagship; I/O 2026 headline, 4x faster output than rival frontier models.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, full set (text + MM), no tools stated; corroborated by Vellum leaderboard (40.2)
GPQA Diamond, measured by Artificial Analysis (cited via BenchLM, which states AA reports 92.2%); Vals AI GPQA mirror reports 92.7%; no official Google number published
MMMU, LayerLens/Stratix independent eval via PricePerToken leaderboard; split (validation) not explicitly stated
GDPval-AA, Elo-style rating for economically valuable knowledge work.
IFBench instruction-following eval, Artificial Analysis independent evaluation (raw score 0.7632653061224, same model-page listing as the tau2-bench-telecom entry above). Not previously tracked.
Also checked HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval logs, LiveBench, Scale Labs, PricePerToken, EvalPlus, non-English blogs, and sibling models for AIME -- MathArena was the only hit.

Google has never published this metric for the model (confirmed absent from its official 14-benchmark eval table across multiple rounds); Artificial Analysis tracks the field but has it null; a competing 91.6% figure from CodingFleet's SEO content was checked and rejected for lacking any named evaluator/methodology and not being corroborated elsewhere.

Distinct from the already-recorded self-reported 'swe-bench-pro' score (55.1%); Google's official eval table includes SWE-bench Pro but not SWE-bench Verified; corroborated to within rounding by CodingFleet's repeated ~79% figure for this model.

Macro success (pass@1 exact match to certified-optimal solutions, 100 hidden test tasks). Wilson 95% CI [19, 36], mean shaped reward 0.704, total eval cost $286. Per-slice exact successes /20: steward_7d 0, rulebook_7d 1, dedup 12, orienteering 2, gauntlet_7g 12. Ended 34% of episodes at budget exhaustion — the field's highest rate, disclosed under the benchmark's >15% prominent-disclosure rule; budget exhaustion is scored as an outcome, not excluded.
Seventh on the NPP anytime ladder. Exact success 0/20, as preregistered for every model.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: launched at Google I/O 2026-05-19 with immediate GA (no waitlist), confirmed via blog.google and deepmind.google/models/gemini/flash/ (both fetched directly). All 4 benchmark numbers (SWE-bench Pro 55.1, MMMU-Pro 83.6, Terminal-Bench 2.1 76.2, CharXiv 84.2) literally confirmed on the cited pages. 1M/64k context, text/image/video/audio input modalities, and $1.50/$9 pricing all confirmed independently. No changes to record.

2026-07-16: Checked all 6 missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench) against (1) Google's own official model-evaluation document (the full results table behind deepmind.google/models/evals-methodology/gemini-3-5-flash, which resolves to https://storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3-5_flash_model_evaluation.pdf and was fetched/read directly) plus the deepmind.google/models/gemini/flash/ and blog.google Gemini 3.5 launch pages — the official table lists exactly 14 benchmarks (Terminal-bench 2.1, SWE-Bench Pro, MCP Atlas, Toolathlon, OSWorld-Verified, Finance Agent v2, GDPval-AA, CharXiv Reasoning, MMMU-Pro, Blueprint-Bench 2, MRCR v2 128k, MRCR v2 1M, HLE, ARC-AGI-2) and none of them is MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or SWE-bench Verified/non-Pro; (2) independent trackers — Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-5-flash), BenchLM.ai (benchlm.ai/models/gemini-3-5-flash), llm-stats.com (explicitly renders these six as unscored footer links, "Score pending"), Vals.ai (vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3.5-flash, which lists MMLU Pro/LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench as tracked categories with no populated score for this model), Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/gemini-3-5-flash, only a composite Epoch Capabilities Index of 155 is shown, no per-benchmark breakdown), and the official LiveCodeBench leaderboard (livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html), which has not been updated past the Gemini-2.5 generation and contains no Gemini 3.x entries at all. This is consistent, multi-source confirmation that Google deliberately did not run/publish these six legacy benchmarks for Gemini 3.5 Flash (they are treated as saturated and superseded by the harder suite above), and no independent lab has back-filled them as of this date. All six gaps are marked omitted_no_evidence; no existing results were altered.

2026-07-17 (round 2, legacy-benchmark deep dive): Re-chased the four still-missing legacy scores (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) using six NEW source types the first pass (2026-07-16) did not check: (1) Stanford HELM -- fetched both HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite, has literal 'MMLU - EM' and 'MATH - Equivalent (CoT)' columns) and HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities, uses MMLU-Pro/Omni-MATH instead) leaderboards directly in-browser and read every model row in both; neither leaderboard has been updated past roughly the Gemini 2.0 Flash / GPT-4o era (Lite) or Gemini 3 Pro (Preview) / GPT-5.1 era (Capabilities) -- no Gemini 3.1 or 3.5 model of any kind appears in either, so HELM has no data for this model at all. (2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar baseline tables -- found and read the UrduMMLU paper (arxiv.org/pdf/2606.07167), which does evaluate 'Gemini-3.5-Flash' but on a translated Urdu-language variant benchmark, not the standard 57-subject English MMLU our 'mmlu' slug tracks, so it's not usable; downloaded and grepped the July-2026 Seed2.0 model card (arxiv.org/pdf/2607.00248, 87pp) which compares against 'Gemini-3-Pro' and 'Gemini-3-Flash' throughout but contains zero mentions of 'Gemini-3.5' or '3.5 Flash' anywhere; spot-checked DeepSeek-V4, Kimi K3, and GLM-5 coverage, none yielded a specific MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for this model. (3) GitHub eval-harness logs -- ran `gh search code` for combinations of gemini-3.5-flash/gemini-3-5-flash with mmlu, humaneval, aime, and MATH-500; zero results combining the model identifier with any of the four benchmark names. (4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- checked OpenRouter's model page (benchmarks section present but empty), Vals.ai (google_gemini-3.5-flash page lists 'MMLU Pro' and other categories all at a flat 0.0%, i.e. tracked-but-unrun, consistent with the first pass's note), PricePerToken/LayerLens's three dedicated leaderboards at /leaderboards/benchmark/{mmlu,math,humaneval} (79, and similar counts of, models each; confirmed via direct page-text inspection that Gemini 3.5 Flash is absent from all three, even though this exact source does cover this exact model for MMMU elsewhere in this file), EvalPlus's HumanEval/HumanEval+ leaderboard (evalplus.github.io -- confirmed stale, top entries are all O1/GPT-4o/DeepSeek-V3-era 2024 models, no Gemini 3.x of any kind), Scale Labs / SEAL (labs.scale.com/leaderboard -- has live 'gemini-3.5-flash' entries for MCP Atlas and DrugDiscoveryBench but the platform's benchmark set is entirely proprietary, no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval category exists), LiveBench.ai (livebench.ai -- has a live 'Gemini 3.5 Flash High' row with real Overall/Reasoning/Coding/Mathematics/etc. scores, but by design LiveBench does not use MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval at all; confirmed by drilling into subtask columns: its Mathematics category breaks down into 'AMPS Hard / integrals with game / math comp / olympiad', and Coding breaks down into 'code generation / code completion' -- none of which is AIME, MATH-500, or HumanEval), and MathArena.ai (matharena.ai -- an ETH Zurich SRI Lab / INSAIT-run rigorous math-competition eval site; this IS the one hit, see the new 'aime' result added above for AIME 2026; it does not run MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval at all, and does not have a Gemini 3.5 Flash entry on its separate AIME 2025 leaderboard). (5) Non-English sources -- searched Chinese ('MMLU 跑分'), Korean (Brunch, Threads, Elancer, namu.wiki, Stork.AI), and Japanese queries; every hit that discussed benchmark numbers only recapped the same official MMMU-Pro/CharXiv/Terminal-Bench/MCP Atlas/GDPval-AA figures already in this file -- no new MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers surfaced in any language; separately opened OpenCompass's Chinese leaderboard (rank.opencompass.org.cn) but its default view did not expose a Gemini-3.5-Flash row and further UI/API drill-down was not fruitful in the time available. (6) Sibling-model comparison tables -- checked Google's own Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite model-evaluation PDF (storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3-1_flash-lite_model_evaluation.pdf, published March 2026, i.e. before Gemini 3.5 Flash existed, so it can't and doesn't include it; also notable: even Google's own methodology there reports 'Multilingual MMLU', not the standard MMLU, so Google appears to have stopped reporting standard MMLU generationally); confirmed via multiple July-2026 news searches that Gemini 3.5 Pro has not yet shipped as of today (missed three launch deadlines, most recently 2026-07-16), so no newer Google sibling model-card exists that could include a Gemini-3.5-Flash baseline row either. Net result: one credible new score recovered (aime, via MathArena -- added above); mmlu, math, and humaneval remain genuinely unscored anywhere checked across all 6 new source-type categories (5 of 6 checked with concrete negative evidence, 1 -- non-English -- partially checked). Also explicitly noted and rejected as non-credible: automatio.ai's model page lists suspiciously round figures (MMLU 89%, MMLU Pro 83%, MATH 88%, AIME 2025 68%, HumanEval 92%) with no named evaluator, no methodology, and a templated 'X scored Y% on this benchmark' phrasing repeated identically across every benchmark row -- consistent with AI-generated SEO filler content rather than a real measurement, so none of those numbers were used per the 'never invent or estimate a score' rule.

2026-07-18 (round 3, final legacy-benchmark pass -- mmlu/math/humaneval): Chased all three remaining gaps using the 5 NEW source-type categories specified for this round (distinct from rounds 1-2's official materials, trackers, HELM, arXiv, GitHub eval-harness logs, MathArena, LiveBench, OpenRouter, Scale SEAL, non-English sources, and sibling-model cards). (1) WAYBACK MACHINE -- genuinely attempted but functionally inaccessible this session: the archive.org "available" API confirmed a real snapshot exists for the deepmind.google/models/gemini/flash/ launch page (capture timestamp 20260519232307), but every attempt to actually render that snapshot's content -- 10+ curl requests to web.archive.org (both the /web/<ts>/ playback path and the /cdx/search/cdx API) spread across ~5 minutes with exponential backoff, plus a fallback attempt via archive.ph/archive.today -- returned persistent HTTP 429 (nginx rate-limit) or a bot-challenge interstitial; the WebFetch tool itself also hard-refuses the web.archive.org host ("unable to fetch"); a Memento aggregator (timetravel.mementoweb.org) was unreachable entirely (connection failure, no route). This is recorded as inconclusive/blocked-by-infrastructure, not a confirmed absence, but no benchmark table content could be extracted via this channel despite a genuine good-faith attempt. (2) CONFERENCE TALKS/KEYNOTES -- checked Google I/O 2026 recap material: blog.google's "100 things we announced at Google I/O 2026", Sundar Pichai's opening-keynote blog post, the AINews/latent.space I/O recap newsletter, buildfastwithai's I/O blog, and the Duke Digital Media Community recap -- all of these only restate the same official benchmark set (Terminal-Bench 2.1, MCP Atlas, GDPval-AA, CharXiv, etc.); none contains a verbally-stated or slide-shown MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure. (3) INVESTOR/PRESS MATERIALS -- fetched Alphabet's June 2026 investor presentation (blog.google/alphabet/investor-presentation-june-2026/) directly: it names Gemini 3.5 Flash and describes its focus areas but contains zero specific benchmark scores of any kind (no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval, and no mention of the model's own tracked benchmarks either); reviewed the Q1 2026 Alphabet earnings call transcript/summary (also benchmark-free); confirmed the Q2 2026 earnings call is scheduled for 2026-07-23 -- after today's 2026-07-18 cutoff -- so no newer investor disclosure exists yet to check. (4) GITHUB ISSUES/SDK REPOS -- ran `gh search issues`, `gh search prs`, and `gh search code` across many query combinations of gemini-3.5-flash with mmlu/math/humaneval/aime; found dozens of integration bug reports (context-window fallback, thought_signature schema errors, missing-from-catalog issues in gemini-cli, LibreChat, cline, litellm, opencode) but zero maintainer-confirmed benchmark numbers. Notably found two independent third-party GitHub catalog/research files that explicitly and independently concluded the same negative: abhijithreddy05/model-catalog's gemini-3.5-flash.txt lists mmlu, math, humaneval, and gpqa all as "not published" with a dash for value; and markusstrasser/agent-infra's dated research doc (gemini-3.5-flash-benchmarks.md, written launch day 2026-05-19) explicitly flags "MMLU-Pro and GPQA Diamond -- absent from Google's official 3.5 Flash card ... Google appears to have moved off MMLU-Pro / GPQA as headline benchmarks for the 3.5 generation." A third repo, Sero01/ai-agents-guide's weekly_run_2026-07-12.md, does list numbers for mmlu_pro/humaneval/math_500/etc., but the author explicitly labels the entire row "**Estimated**" (as opposed to the officially-published Terminal-Bench/SWE-bench figures listed alongside it) -- i.e., self-disclosed as not a real measurement, so not usable. Several other repos found in the code search (Akashkunwar/AI-Beacon, rush86999/atom, Anakin7n/PolicyFlow, just-every/ensemble) contain gemini-3.5-flash MMLU/HumanEval/MATH numbers, but these are all clearly synthetic seed/demo data for unrelated personal apps (files literally named seed_models_db.py, benchmarkData.ts, model_profiles.py) with no named evaluator or methodology -- rejected per the same standard applied to automatio.ai in round 2. (5) THIRD-PARTY REVIEW/ANALYSIS SITES -- fetched Simon Willison's dedicated launch-day writeup (simonwillison.net/2026/May/19/gemini-35-flash/) in full: covers pricing, context window, knowledge cutoff, and platform positioning in detail but contains no benchmark scores of any kind, MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval or otherwise; searched for interconnects.ai (Nathan Lambert) coverage specifically -- found only passing, non-benchmark mentions of Gemini 3.5 in an unrelated "which models I use" post, no dedicated review; fetched digitalapplied.com's "Gemini 3.5 Flash, 5 Days In: Independent Eval Roundup" -- explicitly confirms "the article does not mention specific MMLU, MATH, or AIME scores for Gemini 3.5 Flash, despite those being standard LLM benchmarks"; re-fetched and re-confirmed automatio.ai's benchmark table is the identical previously-rejected filler (MMLU 89%, MATH 88%, AIME 2025 68%, HumanEval 92%, all suspiciously round with no named evaluator) that keeps resurfacing in search-engine summaries -- still rejected, now further undermined by two independent GitHub sources stating these are genuinely "not published"; also fetched the newly-discovered official model-card URL deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-5-flash/ (a different URL structure from the deepmind.google/models/gemini/flash/ page checked in rounds 1-2) and confirmed it lists the identical 14-benchmark set with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval entry. NET RESULT: 4 of 5 new source-type categories checked with clear, consistent negative findings (plus two independent corroborating third-party sources explicitly stating "not published"); the 5th (Wayback Machine) was genuinely attempted but blocked by persistent infrastructure rate-limiting/bot-protection rather than yielding a clean negative. Across three independent research rounds now (2026-07-16, 2026-07-17, 2026-07-18) spanning 15+ distinct source categories, no credible published score for mmlu, math, or humaneval has surfaced for Gemini 3.5 Flash from Google or any independent evaluator. All three gaps are marked omitted_no_evidence; no existing results were altered or added.

2026-07-18 (round 4, livecodebench + swe-bench targeted chase): Closed both remaining CURRENT-generation coding-benchmark gaps with credible, dated, independently-measured scores from Vals.ai, using 5 of the 6 specified research methods. (1) Official launch materials re-checked once more: deepmind.google/models/gemini/flash/, deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-5-flash/, and blog.google's Gemini 3.5 launch post all still list only SWE-bench Pro (55.1%, already recorded) among coding benchmarks -- no LiveCodeBench or SWE-bench Verified figure appears anywhere in Google's official 14-benchmark table, reconfirming round-1's finding. (2) Independent trackers: fetched and raw-parsed Artificial Analysis's model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-5-flash), locating its embedded JSON model record via the literal slug "gemini-3-5-flash" (verified authentic: its gpqa=0.9222 and ifbench=0.7633 fields match this file's existing gpqa/ifbench entries exactly). That record has an explicit `livecodebench` field whose value is `null` (tracked but unmeasured for this model) and contains zero occurrences of any SWE-bench-related field anywhere (not tracked at all). BenchLM.ai's page states no LiveCodeBench score is listed. llm-stats.com's dedicated model page only links to /benchmarks/livecodebench and /benchmarks/swe-bench-verified as bare footer nav items with no score rendered for this model (a spot-check "88.6%" text on the same page turned out to belong to a Claude Opus 4.8 blurb, not this model). Vals.ai, however, yielded real per-benchmark leaderboard data for both metrics once the dedicated leaderboard pages (not the JS-chart-only per-model overview page, which renders a useless flat "0.0%" placeholder for every metric) were fetched and their embedded JSON payload inspected directly: vals.ai/benchmarks/lcb ("Our Implementation of the LiveCodeBench benchmark", updated 2026-07-09) gives google/gemini-3.5-flash Overall 87.6% (Easy 99.1%/Medium 90.6%/Hard 73.1%); vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench (public dataset, updated 2026-07-17) gives Gemini 3.5 Flash Overall 78.80% with a resolution-time difficulty breakdown (<15 min 87%, 15m-1h 78%, 1-4hr 52%). Both added above. (3) LiveCodeBench's own official leaderboard (livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html) is still stuck on a client-side "Loading leaderboard data..." state with no Gemini 3.x entries reachable, reconfirming it hasn't been updated past the 2.5 generation. (4) Sibling/comparison-site corroboration: CodingFleet's Gemini-3.5-Flash comparison articles (e.g. codingfleet.com/blog/gpt-5-4-vs-gemini-3-5-flash/) independently cite a "~79% SWE-bench Verified" figure that corroborates Vals.ai's 78.8% to within rounding, but a separately-surfaced "91.6% LiveCodeBench" figure repeated across CodingFleet's pages does not match Vals.ai's 87.6% and carries no named evaluator or methodology, so it was rejected (the properly-sourced, dated Vals.ai figure was used instead) -- same standard applied to the automatio.ai filler content rejected in round 2. (5) Kilo Code's coding-benchmark page (kilo.ai/models/google-gemini-3-5-flash) lists several AA-sourced coding metrics for this model (AA Coding Index, SciCode, TerminalBench Hard, LCR, IFBench) but explicitly has no LiveCodeBench entry. Net result: both gaps closed with real independent (Vals.ai) scores; Google itself still has not published either metric for this model as of 2026-07-18.
